summary: Feature 006 should be split around shared validation/registry ownership, authoring service orchestration, local API routing, tool registration/rendering, and phase-policy enforcement. Desktop frontend changes are optional and should not be assigned by default. All mutating paths must preserve guide/list/inspect/validate/create/update/duplicate/delete capability, route persistence through WorkflowTemplateRegistryService, and prove no-write behavior for invalid, stale, builtin-protected, ambiguous, denied, and desktop-server-failure cases.

write_sets.reason: Owns extraction of current validation semantics from registry/API into one shared module while preserving registry persistence, unknown-field merge behavior, malformed-config no-write behavior, builtin id protection, linear-only rejection, issue codes, and cache invalidation. This is a dependency for authoring service and API parity work.

Owns operation orchestration, result envelope, basisHash computation, create/update/duplicate/delete no-write paths, builtin copy naming, and registry re-read-before-write behavior. It consumes T001 validation and is consumed by API and tool work.

Owns local authoring endpoint routing, HTTP status mapping, API validation parity, and existing manual endpoint compatibility. Must not diverge from shared validation or bypass authoring service for the new POST /api/workflows/templates/authoring contract.

Owns deterministic static field guide and drift tests for required field groups, allowed completion/transition values, unsupported workflow shapes, and repairable issue codes. It may read shared validation constants from T001; merge order should avoid duplicate constants.

Owns operation-level workflow phase policy for template-authoring mutations. Must not add the whole tool name to getWorkflowPhaseDisallowedTools in a way that blocks read-only guide/list/inspect/validate operations.

Owns new global built-in tool registration, input/output schema, metadata methods, permission classifier text, desktop-server delegation when CC_JIANGXIA_DESKTOP_SERVER_URL is configured, transcript rendering, and tool tests. src/Tool.ts should be read-only unless the existing Tool interface cannot express the required metadata.

Optional only. Assign this task only if implementation deliberately exposes authoring endpoint types to the desktop frontend or changes Settings Workflows behavior. The approved plan does not require a desktop UI redesign or desktop client changes.

shared_surfaces.notes: Single join point for importing WorkflowTemplateAuthoringTool and adding it to getAllBaseTools. Avoid concurrent edits here from service/API tasks.
Storage boundary and cache invalidation owner. Other tasks may read types and list/write semantics but must not introduce alternate persistence or duplicate validation logic.
API route join point for existing manual endpoints and new authoring endpoint. Keep manual endpoint compatibility while sharing service/validation logic.
Policy join point. Mutating authoring operations must be denied by operation policy while read-only operations stay available.
Optional desktop client join point. Do not touch unless frontend code needs the internal authoring endpoint; Settings refetch should continue using existing list/get/create/update/delete methods.

packet_guardrails.rule: All persisted workflow template mutations must call WorkflowTemplateRegistryService.writeTemplates; do not create a separate file writer, localStorage key, tool-private store, or direct writes to protected state.
Validation semantics and issue codes must be shared from the validation module or its constants; do not copy/paste a second validator into the tool or API.

Invalid, malformed, stale, denied, ambiguous, missing-target, builtin update/delete, builtin id shadowing, and desktop endpoint failure paths must return diagnostics with persisted:false and leave workflow config unchanged.

Builtin templates must not be directly updated, deleted, or shadowed. Builtin modification intent is duplicate-to-user first, using agent-development-custom, then -2, -3 naming when target fields are omitted.

Update and delete require selector.source=user plus basisHash from list/inspect. Re-read the registry immediately before write and reject mismatches with WORKFLOW_TEMPLATE_STALE_BASIS and nextAction inspect-and-retry.

When CC_JIANGXIA_DESKTOP_SERVER_URL is present, mutating tool operations must use the local server authoring endpoint. If that endpoint fails or is unavailable, return a visible failed/no-write result and do not silently direct-write from the child process.

Global tool registration must respect active workflow phase policy at the operation level. Read-only operations remain available; create/update/duplicate/delete are denied in disallowed active phases.

Do not call WorkflowRuntimeService or mutate active/historical workflow session snapshots, transcripts, provider config, MCP config, skills, plugin state, OAuth tokens, adapters, teams, or session records.

Do not force desktop UI/API client changes into the implementation packet. Create T007 only if desktop source is intentionally touched; otherwise leave desktop files read-only and rely on existing Settings refetch.

Do not remove or defer any first-scope operation: guide, list, inspect, validate, create, update, duplicate, delete. Surface minimization may consolidate operations only inside workflow_template_authoring.

desktop_optionality.notes: Desktop UI/client changes are not required by the approved plan. The compatibility requirement is satisfied by the root local server authoring endpoint plus existing Settings Workflows refetch/list behavior. If any file under desktop/src is touched, add same-area desktop tests where behavior changes and run bun run check:desktop.

verification_expectations.notes: Prove shared validation preserves current registry/API-required issue codes, unknown-field preservation, malformed-config write blocking, builtin id conflict, duplicate id handling, required phase fields, required output artifacts, handoff fields, transition authority, and unsupported linear-only shape rejection.

Cover guide/list/inspect/validate/create/update/duplicate/delete result envelopes, SHA-256 basisHash stability, stale update/delete no-write, builtin copy naming, user conflict rejection, malformed existing config rejection, and session snapshot non-mutation by absence of runtime-service writes.

Cover POST /api/workflows/templates/authoring success and handled rejection envelopes, non-2xx malformed request handling, existing manual endpoint compatibility, cache-owning server writes, and Settings-compatible list/refetch after mutation.

Assert guide includes required field groups, allowed completion criteria types, transition authorities, unsupported parallel/nested/branch/loop shapes, and repair hints mapped to validation issue codes.

Prove read-only operations are not denied, mutating operations are denied in non-implementation active phases and verification by default, and mutating operations are allowed outside workflows or in implementation/implement phases.

Cover tool schema, isReadOnly, isDestructive delete-only, isConcurrencySafe false, global registration, permission/classifier summaries, phase-policy validation, direct service path, desktop-server delegation path, no fallback on endpoint failure, and transcript-auditable result rendering.
Run only if desktop source is touched. Verify Settings Workflows still lists/refetches templates and no desktop UI redesign or separate store is introduced.
Final implementation handoff must include changed files, tests added, final quality report path, coverage report path, pass/fail/skip counts, and explicit live/desktop-server blockers if any.

notes_for_leader: Recommended dependency order: T001 first, then T002, then T003/T005/T004 in parallel where merge timing allows, then T006 integration, with T007 omitted unless desktop source is deliberately touched.
Join owner should sequence edits to src/tools.ts, workflowTemplateRegistryService.ts, workflowTemplates.ts, and workflowToolPolicy.ts to avoid parallel merge collisions.
The current code already has duplicate validation logic in workflowTemplateRegistryService.ts and workflowTemplates.ts; T001 should collapse this before T002/T003 finalize behavior.
The existing desktop WorkflowTemplateManager and WorkflowTemplateEditor already consume manual endpoints; do not require them to call the internal authoring endpoint unless product scope changes.

Current src/Tool.ts already supports isReadOnly, isDestructive, isConcurrencySafe, validateInput, checkPermissions, rendering, and max result size; treat it as read-only unless a concrete type gap appears.
Do not let a packet satisfy registration by adding the new tool only to workflow-scoped tools; the plan requires global ordinary-chat availability with operation-level policy gating.
Forbidden paths include desktop/src/** by default for non-T007 packets; if T007 is activated, narrow the write scope to the exact desktop files listed in its write set and require check:desktop.
Every packet should read plan.md, plan-contract.json, quickstart.md, workflow-authoring-tool.md, workflow-authoring-api.md, and its listed source references before editing.
